commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the legacy doctor config migration to copy Mattermost root DM/group access-policy keys into existing named accounts when seeding `accounts.default`, with regression tests for migration and account inheritance.

PR surface: Source +29, Tests +87. Total +116 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main removes Mattermost top-level policy into `accounts.default`, while Mattermost named accounts previously inherited those root fields through account merge. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is appropriate because this is a maintainer-labeled draft with a compatibility-sensitive migration and no narrow automated repair blocker.

target.title: Fix Mattermost account policy migration
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw 2026.6.10-beta.1 could migrate Mattermost configs with top-level access policy into `channels.mattermost.accounts.default` only. Existing named accounts that previously inherited `dmPolicy`, `allowFrom`, `groupPolicy`, and `groupAllowFrom` then lost their effective access policy, causing Mattermost group/channel messages to be dropped before routing with `mattermost: drop group message (no group allowlist)`.

## Why This Change Was Made

The shared doctor migration removes selected single-account keys from the channel root when it seeds `accounts.default`. Mattermost named accounts rely on those root access-policy keys as inherited defaults before migration, so the migration now copies Mattermost inherited policy keys into named accounts that do not already define their own values before removing the root keys.

Existing account overrides are preserved because the migration only fills missing own fields.

## User Impact

Users updating legacy Mattermost configs with multiple named accounts keep the same effective DM and group access policy after doctor/config migration. Named accounts like `tony` and `research` continue accepting previously allowed Mattermost group/channel traffic without manual per-account policy edits.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-legacy-config.migrations.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/accounts.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm build`
